official.name: James E. Clyburn
official.office_address: 274 Cannon House Office Building Washington DC 20515-4006

summary.narrative: Across his commitments on broadband, healthcare, housing, education, jobs, and protecting Social Security/Medicare, Clyburn's record this term is dominated by sustained legislative sponsorship and appropriations advocacy. Broadband shows the most advanced delivery, with enacted federal benefits and a launched statewide deployment effort; healthcare shows secured funding and enrollment assistance, while housing, education, and jobs largely consist of introduced bills that remained in committee under a Congress not aligned with his proposals. He took documented protective action on Social Security and Medicare with no documented cuts enacted.
summary.grade_rationale: The record reflects consistent, well-documented action across most promises but uneven completion: only broadband reaches an advanced/launched stage, while several economic and education pledges stall at the bill-introduction milestone, and his causal role is frequently shared (0.4-0.6) given Congress and federal agencies as necessary co-actors. Promise quality is mostly directional-to-specific (clarity 3-4) with moderate-to-high magnitude.

data_gaps: Promise candidates are largely directional restatements drawn from a 2024 election article and a 2026 campaign-announcement list (c1-c12), with no campaign-window-dated original text; near-duplicate 2024/2026 pledges were consolidated. Many delivery evidence items predate the current term and reflect Clyburn's long tenure rather than this-term action — reviewer should confirm term attribution. The jobs promise relies on a campaign-site claim (c227) flagged for credit overclaim. Some sources (c200 GovTrack fetch failure) were unusable.

provisional_status.display: Grade is record-to-date — term in progress, 98.4% elapsed (as of 2026-06-23). It will change as the term continues.
provisional_status.note: Distinct from any per-promise 'PROVISIONAL (early-term)' flag in scorecard.card_flags, which concerns a single promise's own deadline, not the term's completeness.
